name: Christian Watson
headline: Big-play WR1 with vacated targets and breakout volume
body:
Even on a part-time route share, Watson's 2025 tape pops. In 10 games he posted a 0.587 EPA per play and a 61.8% success rate while running only 43.7% of routes, turning 55 targets into 611 yards and six scores at 17.5 per catch. His 22.8% air-yards share and 17.76 air yards per target confirm a true downfield role, and 2.7 yards per route run ranks among the league's best. The drop rate sat at 1.8%, with 11 grabs of 20-plus yards in a half-season.

The path to a leap is volume. With Romeo Doubs gone in free agency and Dontayvion Wicks traded, the Packers' target hierarchy clears for Watson as Jordan Love's primary read and designed red-zone option. His route share lived near 44%; pushing that toward a full workload on his efficiency profile makes him a genuine WR2 with WR1 ceiling. He's worth a Round 6 swing, with the only real caveat being the injury history that cost him seven weeks last year.
